tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_select_chain_non_pointer(i32 %X, i32 %Y) {
entry:
  %XeqC = icmp eq i32 %X, 0
  %YeqC = icmp eq i32 %Y, 0
  %either = select i1 %XeqC, i1 true, i1 %YeqC
  %both = select i1 %XeqC, i1 %YeqC, i1 false
  %XeqY = icmp eq i32 %X, %Y
  %equal = select i1 %either, i1 %both, i1 %XeqY
  ret i1 %equal
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary specifically mentions Swift optional pointer equality pattern with i64. This tests the same select-chain folding with i32 integers, exercising setSameSign(false) fix on non-pointer types.

  %either = select i1 %XeqC, i1 true, i1 %YeqC

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_select_equality_inequality_mixed(i64 %X, i64 %Y) {
entry:
  %XneC = icmp ne i64 %X, 0
  %YneC = icmp ne i64 %Y, 0
  %either = select i1 %XneC, i1 false, i1 %YneC
  %both = select i1 %XneC, i1 %YneC, i1 true
  %XeqY = icmp eq i64 %X, %Y
  %equal = select i1 %either, i1 %both, i1 %XeqY
  ret i1 %equal
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes Swift optional pointer equality with specific icmp eq comparisons to 0. This test uses icmp ne comparisons to 0 with inverted select logic, representing a semantically equivalent but structurally different pattern that benefits from the setSameSign(false) fix.

alive2.src:
define i1 @test_select_equality_inequality_mixed(i64 %X, i64 %Y) {
entry:
  %XneC = icmp ne i64 %X, 0
  %YneC = icmp ne i64 %Y, 0
  %either = select i1 %XneC, i1 false, i1 %YneC
  %both = select i1 %XneC, i1 %YneC, i1 true
  %XeqY = icmp eq i64 %X, %Y
  %equal = select i1 %either, i1 %both, i1 %XeqY
  ret i1 %equal
}

entry:
  %XneC.not = icmp eq i64 %X, 0
  %YneC = icmp ne i64 %Y, 0
  %either = select i1 %XneC.not, i1 %YneC, i1 false
  %XeqY = icmp eq i64 %X, %Y
  %equal = select i1 %either, i1 true, i1 %XeqY
  ret i1 %equal
}
